notes: Each task is intended for a single agent to implement end-to-end.
Each task includes explicit files to read and files allowed to modify.
Each task requires the agent to fill in session_log.start and session_log.end before merging.

tasks.title: Mountinfo: choose greatest mount id for duplicate mountpoints
tasks.goal: Implement the C0-spec requirement: if multiple /proc/self/mountinfo entries match the same mountpoint, select the entry with the greatest numeric mount id.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: two mountinfo lines with same decoded mountpoint selects the highest mount id.
No other behavior changes in mountinfo parser beyond this selection rule.
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Read C0-spec requirements for mountinfo selection. Update crates/world/src/mountinfo.rs so overlay_backing_dirs_for_mount_point_from_str considers *all* matching mountpoint entries and selects the one with the greatest mount id (numeric). Add a fixture-style unit test that includes at least two matching entries with different mount ids and differing upper/work dirs; assert the higher mount id wins. Keep the parsing Linux-only and minimal; do not add unrelated features.

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-001 — <agent_name> — begin mount id selection work
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-001 — <agent_name> — summary + commands run + test results

tasks.title: Mountinfo: decode escape sequences in upperdir/workdir/lowerdir values
tasks.goal: Implement the C0-spec requirement: decode mountinfo octal escapes (\\040, \\011, \\012, \\134) in overlay backing dir values, not just mountpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: escaped upperdir/workdir decode correctly (include at least one \\040 case).
If lowerdir contains multiple entries, each component is decoded consistently.
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Read the C0-spec decoding rules. In crates/world/src/mountinfo.rs, ensure decode_mountinfo_path (or a new helper) is applied to the parsed overlay super_options values for upperdir/workdir and each lowerdir component. Add unit tests that cover decoding in these values, including a multi-lowerdir case.

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-002 — <agent_name> — begin overlay backing dir value decoding
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-002 — <agent_name> — summary + commands run + test results

tasks.title: Mountinfo: fail-closed API when overlay upperdir/workdir missing
tasks.goal: Align crates/world mountinfo helper API with spec: when the project mountpoint is overlay but missing upperdir/workdir, return a deterministic error (not None/partial).
acceptance_criteria: Introduce a Result-returning helper (or adjust existing) that errors when fstype!=overlay or upperdir/workdir missing.
Unit tests cover: non-overlay mountpoint, overlay missing upperdir, overlay missing workdir.
No broadening of allowlists or behavior outside mountinfo parsing.
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Implement a strict overlay backing-dir derivation helper in crates/world/src/mountinfo.rs that returns Result<OverlayBackingDirs> for a given mountpoint, and errors when fstype != overlay or either upperdir/workdir is missing. Add fixture tests for those error cases. Keep the error messages high-signal (include mountpoint + missing key / wrong fstype).

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-003 — <agent_name> — begin strict mountinfo API work
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-003 — <agent_name> — summary + commands run + test results

tasks.title: world-agent internal_exec: require overlay backing dirs and fail closed (exit 4) under spec conditions
tasks.goal: Align crates/world-agent/src/internal_exec.rs with C0-spec: when full isolation + writable + Landlock supported + Landlock intended, derivation failures must abort exec with exit code 4 and a high-signal diagnostic.
acceptance_criteria: Mountinfo strict API returns a strict type (upperdir/workdir are non-optional) so callers cannot accidentally ignore required fields.
When Landlock is supported and allowlists are set for full isolation + writable, mountinfo derivation failure exits 4 with an actionable message (mountpoint + reason).
When Landlock is unsupported, behavior does not introduce a new failure mode (no fail-closed solely due to lack of Landlock).
No snapshot schema/hash changes; no new user-facing config keys.
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Fold-in note: eliminate the Option footgun by making the strict mountinfo helper return a strict type with non-optional upperdir/workdir. Then update crates/world-agent/src/internal_exec.rs to compute whether derivation is required per C0-spec (full isolation + writable + Landlock supported + allowlists present). If required, call the strict mountinfo helper and, on error, print a high-signal diagnostic and exit 4 before executing the inner command. Ensure that Landlock unsupported does not create a new failure mode. Keep diagnostics structured (JSON is OK) and do not change policy snapshot behavior.

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-004 — <agent_name> — begin internal_exec fail-closed alignment
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-004 — <agent_name> — summary + commands run + test results

tasks.title: Tests: strengthen world-agent integration coverage for allowlist + deny semantics (non-PTY)
tasks.goal: Ensure the behavior is locked in by tests: allowlisted writes succeed; non-allowlisted writes are denied; failures are actionable when prerequisites are missing.
acceptance_criteria: Existing allowlist test continues to pass on systems with overlay + Landlock.
Add at least one targeted assertion that would have caught the prior drift (e.g., Landlock not applying silently or derivation failing without a clear error).
Tests remain gated appropriately when overlay/Landlock unavailable (no new flakiness).
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Review the existing non-PTY full isolation tests and update them to better detect regressions relevant to this feature: (1) allowlisted write succeeds, (2) denied write is blocked, and (3) if Landlock is expected but cannot be applied/derived, the failure is explicit and high-signal. Keep tests resilient by gating on overlay/Landlock availability where necessary.

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-005 — <agent_name> — begin integration test hardening
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-005 — <agent_name> — summary + commands run + test results

tasks.title: Planning pack hygiene: reflect completion + remove ADR placeholder
tasks.goal: Bring docs into alignment with the now-passing CI behavior and remove template placeholders that should not ship.
acceptance_criteria: Remove the leftover ADR template placeholder line for ADR_BODY_SHA256 instructions (retain the actual hash line).
Update planning-pack task statuses to reflect completed integration tasks once evidence (CI run URLs/ids) is recorded.
Fill C0-closeout_report.md with the CI run URLs/ids that show Linux + macOS passing.
tasks.kickoff_prompt: Update the planning artifacts to match reality now that Linux+macOS pass in CI: remove the ADR template placeholder line in ADR-0015, populate C0-closeout_report.md with the passing CI run URLs/ids, and update docs/project_management/next/full-isolation-landlock-overlayfs-compat/tasks.json statuses for C0-integ-* / C0-integ accordingly. Keep edits narrowly scoped to these files; do not alter technical decisions/spec language unless explicitly required.

session_log.start: START — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-006 — <agent_name> — begin planning pack hygiene updates
session_log.end: END — <UTC_TIMESTAMP> — FI-006 — <agent_name> — summary + links to recorded CI runs
